title: Bus message envelope
description: The on-disk format of one bus message. Owned by tools/bus.py; this schema is the canonical definition of the envelope.

id.description: Unique message id. A reply references it via in_reply_to; there is no separate request id.

to.description: Recipient: an agent id (directed), or `*` (everyone / broadcast).

in_reply_to.description: The id of the request this message answers. Present only on a reply.

notify_user.description: Present and true only when the sending agent intends the user to see this.

operator_origin.description: Present and true only when the message originates from the operator.

body.description: The payload. Optional; any type. A standard request's body is a fixed identifier string (e.g. "identity", "status") that the recipient's sidecar answers itself.
